unpinned: R2 active-path cursor metadata-cache sync (active==beadID -> phase.DerivePhase -> syncPhase) is not unit-pinned: runRelease calls phase.DerivePhase directly (unseamed), so only the NON-active path (no syncPhase) is asserted. Acknowledged in release_test.go (TestReleaseCursorRewindOnlyWhenActive) and acceptable: the load-bearing correctness is set-open's self-rewind of the DERIVED cursor (ADR-0023), which IS pinned; syncPhase only mirrors the mindspec_phase cache.
summary: Whole-spec suite adequately pins R1-R4 with genuine RED-pins; hermetic and CI-runnable; the cobra-drift guard is green and now covers the new release verb. Full touched suite (cmd/mindspec, next, doctor, executor, gitutil, redact) PASSES (count=1, 280s); the executor cwd-recovery test also passes under -race. R1: SelectWorkByName('b' from [a,b,c]) returns b (named-not-items[0], RED on pre-fix items[0]); help golden asserts 'claim path'/'claim that bead'; positional+--pick conflict errors with 'exactly one selector'. R2: release_test pins remove-FIRST/set-open-LAST via both happy-path ordering AND a set-open-failure path (proves Remove already ran -> recoverable 'still-claimed'), dirty-refuse-without-force / proceed-with-force-as-pre-gate (dirty-check short-circuited), and defaultSetOpen args (--status=open --assignee=). The cwd-safety AC uses a REAL temp-git repo + a fake whose onRemove ACTUALLY deletes the bead worktree, sits the process inside it, and asserts chdir-back-to-root + a live post-removal stat (the fake-only structurally-cannot-catch footgun is correctly escalated to a real-deletion test). R3: ClaimBead surfaces verbatim 'depends_on_id' stderr and the load-bearing assertion is NOT-contains 'may already be claimed'; doctor adds schema-drift (Warn on schema-signature stderr, OK on healthy, OK/skip on unrelated failure -> no false-warn) and multiple-bd-on-PATH (Warn naming both dirs, OK on single). R4: gitutil DetectDefaultBranch returns 'develop' from symbolic-ref (detected-not-hardcoded), falls THROUGH empty/garbage symbolic-ref to remote-show, and errors when both fail; the executor integration test builds a bare origin on 'develop' with a commit that lives ONLY on origin/develop and asserts the spec branch base == origin/develop tip AND != local main HEAD (defeats hardcoded-main coincidence); no-remote and unreachable-remote both fall back to local HEAD + WARN, never hard-fail. Seams are clean: runBDCombFn, runBDFn, execCommand/swapExec/swapExecFunc, newRepoExecutor + fakeWorktreeOps, stubBd/stubBdFail/fakeBdDir, buildMindspecBinary, bdRunCombinedForRelease, releaseDeps fakes -- no test depends on internal/harness, a real bd, or the network (bd-requiring executor tests t.Skip when bd absent). Only gap: the active-path syncPhase mirror is integration-only (non-active path is pinned; derived cursor self-rewind via set-open is pinned), which is acceptable for a final gate.
